metadata.title: Madelung Exceptions — 3d Transition Metals

beats.narration_text: Kumusta. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of madelung exceptions. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of madelung exceptions

beats.narration_text: Madelung Exceptions is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Exchange energy drives Cr and Cu away from naive aufbau predictions. Adjust K to see the threshold.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Madelung Exceptions. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: K (exchange integral):. Verify: Exchange energy drives Cr and Cu away from naive aufbau predictions. Adjust K to see the threshold.. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Madelung Exceptions. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: K (exchange integral):. Verify: Exchange energy dri

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qm2-madelung-exceptions.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: madelung exceptions. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag K (exchange integral): from 0 to 0.40. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of K (exchange integral): from 0 to 0.40

beats.narration_text: Exchange energy drives Cr and Cu away from naive aufbau predictions. Adjust K to see the threshold.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of madelung exceptions. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Madelung Exceptions simulation and add a second slider that controls K (exchange integral):. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Madelung Exceptions simulation and add a second slider that controls K (exchange integral):. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
